ground_truth.title: Exploring the AndroidX WebGPU API in Kotlin
with: To start exploring the WebGPU API, we write a method that retrieves the details of the underlying GPU device that is visible to the API.
To execute a program on the GPU with the WebGPU API, we need to write the program in WGSL (WebGPU Shader Language).
Execution on a GPU device requires memory regions to store inputs and outputs of the program. These memory regions are represented with a GPUBuffer.
To associate these buffers with the shader, we define a GPUBindGroup, where the visibility of each buffer, its type, and index within the group are mentioned.
A GPUComputePipeline represents the sequence of steps performed by a compute shader module.
